query: how many people can play this game?
expectedTextIncludes: 4 or 5 PLAYERS: Use all

query: how many cards does the game come with?
expectedTextIncludes: CONTENTS: 56 CARDS

query: how many cards do I get dealt at the start?
expectedTextIncludes: deal 7 cards face down to each player. Everyone now has a hand of 8 cards total (7 cards + 1 Defuse)

query: what do you do with the leftover Defuse cards after dealing one to everyone?
expectedTextIncludes: Shuffle 2 of the extras back into the deck, and remove the rest from the game.

query: how do you actually win?
expectedTextIncludes: If you don't explode, you win.

query: do you have to play a card every turn or can you just draw straight away?
expectedTextIncludes: Play as many or as few cards as you'd like, then draw a card to end your turn.

query: can you throw down a Nope card when it's not even your turn?
expectedTextIncludes: You can play a Nope at any time before an action has begun, even if it's not your turn.

query: if someone attacks me and I play a Skip, does it get me out of both turns or just one?
expectedTextIncludes: If you play a Skip as a defense to an Attack, it only ends 1 of the 2 turns. 2 Skips would end both turns.

query: when is the game actually over?
expectedTextIncludes: The last Living player wins the game.

query: do you earn points in this game or does the last person alive just win?
expectedTextIncludes: Eventually, every player will explode except for one, who wins the game!

query: How many people can play Catan?
expectedTextIncludes: Place 2 settlements and 2 roads of each color as shown. If only 3 are playing, remove the red pieces.

query: What pieces and cards come inside the Catan box?
expectedTextIncludes: 95 resource cards (bearing the symbols for the brick, grain, lumber, ore, and wool resources)

query: Who goes first and how do you decide the starting player?
expectedTextIncludes: Each player rolls both dice. The player who rolls highest is the starting player and begins.

query: How many resource cards does each player get at the very start of the game?
expectedTextIncludes: Each player receives the 3 resources from the terrain hexes adjacent to their settlement marked by the white star.

query: How many points do you need to win Catan?
expectedTextIncludes: The first player to reach (be at) 10 victory points (VPs) on their turn wins the game.

query: What do you do on your turn and in what order?
expectedTextIncludes:
- Roll for Resource Production Y (the roll applies to all players)

- Trade Y

- Build Y

query: What happens when someone rolls a 7? Do you lose cards if you have too many?
expectedTextIncludes: every player who has more than 7 resource cards must select half (rounded down) of their resource cards and return them to the bank.

query: Can I trade with the bank if no one wants what I have? Is there a minimum trade ratio?
expectedTextIncludes: you can always trade at 4:1 by putting 4 identical resource cards back in their stack and taking any 1 resource card of your choice for it.

query: Does the game end the moment someone hits 10 points, or do you have to wait for your turn?
expectedTextIncludes: If you have-or reach-10 victory points on your turn, the game ends immediately and you win!

query: What gives you points in Catan? What is everything worth?
expectedTextIncludes:
1 settlement = 1 VP

1 city = 2 VPs

Longest Road special card = 2 VPs

Largest Army special card = 2 VPs

Victory point (VP) card = 1 VP

query: how many people can play Monopoly at once?
expectedTextIncludes: When more than five persons play, the Banker may elect to act only as Banker and Auctioneer.

query: what pieces and stuff come in the Monopoly box?
expectedTextIncludes: The equipment consists of a board, 2 dice, tokens, 32 houses and 12 hotels.

query: how much money does each person get at the start?
expectedTextIncludes: Each player is given $1500 divided as follows: 2 each of $500' s, $100' s and $50' s; 6 $20' s; 5 each of $10' s, $5' s and $1' s.

query: in the fast version of the game do you get any property cards to start with?
expectedTextIncludes: the Banker shuffles then deals three Title Deed cards to each player. These are free - no payment to the Bank is required.

query: what is the goal of Monopoly and how do you actually win?
expectedTextIncludes: The object of the game is to become the wealthiest player through buying, renting and selling property.

query: what do you do on your turn in Monopoly?
expectedTextIncludes: throw the dice and move your token in the direction of the arrow the number of spaces indicated by the dice.

query: do I have to pay if I land on another player's space?
expectedTextIncludes: When you land on property owned by another player, the owner collects rent from you in accordance with the list printed on its Title Deed card.

query: how do you get out of jail?
expectedTextIncludes: You get out of Jail by... (1) throwing doubles on any of your next three turns

query: when is a player out of the game for good?
expectedTextIncludes: You are declared bankrupt if you owe more than you can pay either to another player or to the Bank.

query: what if nobody goes broke — is there a way to end the game early and figure out who's ahead?
expectedTextIncludes: agree upon a definite hour of termination, when the richest player will be declared the winner.

query: how many people can play Sushi Go?
expectedTextIncludes: In a 5 player game, deal 7 cards to each player.

query: what cards come in the box?
expectedTextIncludes: 14x Sashimi

query: how many cards does each person get at the start?
expectedTextIncludes: In a 4 player game, deal 8 cards to each player.

query: how do you win Sushi Go?
expectedTextIncludes: Whoever has the most points after 3 rounds is the winner.

query: what actually happens when it's your turn?
expectedTextIncludes: all players simultaneously choose any 1 card from their hands that they would like to keep and place it face-down in front of them

query: what does the chopsticks card actually let you do?
expectedTextIncludes: you may take 2 sushi cards on a future turn!

query: does wasabi triple any card you pick up, or only specific ones?
expectedTextIncludes: this nigiri must be placed on top of the wasabi. This is to show that the nigiri has been dipped in wasabi and has tripled in value!

query: how many rounds does a full game last?
expectedTextIncludes: After the third round is scored, there will be some cards remaining in the draw pile.

query: how do pudding cards score — when do you count them?
expectedTextIncludes: The player with the most pudding cards scores 6 points .

query: how many points do dumplings give you?
expectedTextIncludes: The more dumpling cards you have, the more points you will score

query: how many players can join a game of Quacks of Quedlinburg?
expectedTextIncludes: for 2-4 players ages 10 and up

query: what pieces and components come in the box?
expectedTextIncludes:
4 pots (player boards)

1 Scoring Track with Turn Indicator

215 ingredient chips

query: what chips do I put in my bag at the very start of the game?
expectedTextIncludes: 1 white 3-chip, 1 orange 1-chip and 1 green 1-chip, and puts them into their bag

query: what's the point of the game — what am I actually trying to do?
expectedTextIncludes: players draw from their ingredient bag in the hopes of creating the best potion

query: what does everyone do during a round — how does a turn actually play out?
expectedTextIncludes: everyone draws chip after chip from their bags, placing them on the appropriate numbered space in their pots

query: when does my pot blow up and what happens when it does?
expectedTextIncludes: you have too many cherry bombs in your potion and the pot explodes

query: can I choose to stop pulling chips out of my bag before I explode?
expectedTextIncludes: Once everyone has decided to stop drawing chips, or has been forced to stop because of an explosion

query: how do I earn victory points at the end of a round?
expectedTextIncludes: everyone receives the victory points depicted on their respective scoring space

query: how many rounds does a full game last?
expectedTextIncludes: The game consists of 9 turns.

query: how many new ingredient chips can I buy at the end of a round?
expectedTextIncludes: If buying 2 chips, they cannot be the same color

query: how many people can play Scrabble?
expectedTextIncludes: SCRABBLE® is a word game for 2, 3 or 4 players.

query: what comes in the Scrabble box?
expectedTextIncludes:
100 Magnetic Letter Tiles

- 4 Magnetic Tile Racks

- 1 Tile/Storage Bag

- 1 Playing Board

query: how many tiles do you get at the start of the game?
expectedTextIncludes: Each player, in turn, then draws seven new tiles and places them on their racks.

query: how do you win at Scrabble?
expectedTextIncludes: The object of the game is to get the highest score.

query: what are my options when it's my turn?
expectedTextIncludes: has the choice of exchanging tiles, passing or adding one or more tiles to those already played so as to form new words

query: do I draw new tiles after I play some?
expectedTextIncludes: the player draws as many new tiles as they have played, thus always keeping seven tiles in their rack

query: what happens if I think someone played a fake or made-up word?
expectedTextIncludes: If the word challenged is unacceptable, the player takes back their tiles and loses their turn.

query: when does the game end?
expectedTextIncludes: all the tiles have been drawn and one of the players has used all the tiles in their rack

query: what do the different colored squares on the board do?
expectedTextIncludes: A light red square doubles the score of the word. A dark red square triples the score of the word.

query: is there a bonus for using all seven tiles in one turn?
expectedTextIncludes: Any player who plays all seven of their tiles in a single turn scores a premium of 50 points in addition to their regular score
